title: Cities Reclaim Space from Cars
subtitle: 全球城市正在把街道还给行人和自行车

paragraphs.en: Urban planners worldwide are fundamentally rethinking how cities allocate space to transportation. Streets once dominated by automobiles are being transformed into pedestrian plazas, bicycle lanes, and green corridors. This movement represents a dramatic reversal of decades of car-centric urban development.
paragraphs.zh: 世界各地的城市规划者正在从根本上重新思考城市如何为交通分配空间。曾经被汽车主导的街道正在被改造成步行广场、自行车道和绿色通道。这一运动代表着对数十年以汽车为中心的城市发展的戏剧性逆转。

paragraphs.en: Paris has emerged as a global leader in urban transformation. The city has removed thousands of parking spaces, created hundreds of kilometers of protected bike paths, and banned cars from historic central areas. Mayor Anne Hidalgo's vision of a fifteen-minute city—where all necessities are accessible by walking—inspires planners globally.
paragraphs.zh: 巴黎已成为城市转型的全球领导者。该市已移除数千个停车位，建造了数百公里的受保护自行车道，并禁止汽车进入历史中心区域。市长安妮·伊达尔戈的“十五分钟城市愿景”——所有必需品都可步行到达——正在激励全球的规划者。

paragraphs.en: Health benefits from reduced car dependency extend beyond cleaner air. Active transportation like walking and cycling contributes to physical fitness and mental wellbeing. Studies show that neighborhoods designed for pedestrians foster stronger community connections and local economic activity.
paragraphs.zh: 减少对汽车依赖带来的健康益处不仅限于更清洁的空气。步行和骑自行车等主动交通方式有助于身体健康和心理健康。研究表明，为行人设计的社区能够促进更强的社区联系和本地经济活动。

paragraphs.en: Resistance to car-free initiatives often comes from business owners fearing lost customers. However, evidence consistently shows that pedestrian-friendly streets boost retail sales. People on foot spend more time browsing and make more frequent purchases than those driving past at speed.
paragraphs.zh: 对无车倡议的抵制通常来自担心失去顾客的商家。然而，证据一致表明，对行人友好的街道能够提升零售额。步行的人比快速驾车经过的人花更多时间浏览并更频繁地购买。

paragraphs.en: The pandemic accelerated these trends as cities rapidly created outdoor dining spaces and temporary bike lanes. Many of these emergency measures have become permanent features. Citizens who experienced calmer, greener streets are reluctant to return to the noise and pollution of car-dominated landscapes.
paragraphs.zh: 疫情加速了这些趋势，城市迅速创建了户外用餐空间和临时自行车道。许多紧急措施已成为永久性设施。体验过更安静、更绿色街道的市民不愿回到以汽车为主导的嘈杂和污染的环境。
